provenance.method: Full nine-slide contact-sheet visual review of this run’s LibreOffice-rendered slide images; structural editability assessed separately by inspection.

evidence: Slides 2–8 use clear assertion-style titles, including “Evidence is counted, not implied” (S03) and “Comparability does not require identical rendering” (S07); S01 and S09 form a coherent opening and closing.

Visible claims are internally coherent: S03 reports nine inspected slides and two builders, while S04’s contract table, S05’s capability chart, and S06’s Plan–Build–Inspect flow support the acceptance-evidence narrative. External source/package truth is outside visual review and is checked separately by contracts and inspection.

The nine-slide sequence moves from shared contracts (S02), through counted evidence and native objects (S03–S05), to process/comparability (S06–S08) and the shipping gate (S09); S02, S07, and S08 remain comparatively sparse text-card treatments.

All nine renders maintain consistent margins, title placement, and alignment; S03, S06, and S07 are balanced, while S02, S04, S05, and S08 retain substantial lower-page whitespace and some small secondary labels at contact-sheet scale.

S04’s table is orderly, S05’s chart is legible with direct values, and S06’s numbered process cards are consistent; restrained beige surfaces, thin rules, and plain text-heavy cards keep the result below top-tier Premium polish.

LibreOffice renders for S01–S09 show stable geometry with no visible clipping, overlap, blank page, broken glyph, or malformed component; separate inspection binds this judgment to native text/table/chart and zero whole-slide raster evidence.

notes: Total visual score: 15/18; no zero dimension.
No visible clipping, overlap, blank slide, or LibreOffice render artifact was found in the nine-slide contact sheet.
Scores are model visual judgments bound to the exact deck bytes and render set; automatic metrics are separate corroboration.
